issues.description: Launcher scripts validate socket_path but do not use it when invoking tmux, so socket targeting depends on inherited TMUX environment. Using explicit '-S "$socket_path"' would make isolated-socket behavior deterministic.

codeReview.summary: Foundation requirements are implemented correctly: scaffold exists, Prefix+g namespace is wired, required @workspace_agent_* defaults are set idempotently, user overrides are preserved, and no unintended global -n key remaps were introduced.

issues.description: Use explicit tmux socket targeting via the passed socket_path to avoid relying on inherited TMUX context.
Read/show operations currently rely on default tmux context; prefer '-S "$socket_path"' for deterministic isolated-socket scoping.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission baseline guidance requires asserting both prefix and workspace_agent key tables, but services.yaml test currently asserts only prefix table availability.

summary: Review PASS for foundation scope. The implementation satisfies the feature contract and expected behavior with low regression risk; only non-blocking hardening suggestions were identified around explicit socket targeting and shared validation coverage alignment.
